rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): harden DoD gate, workspace preflight, and rollback UX
rawCommits.body:
Motivated by the `zero-program` crate task that reported
`Committed 2044864` / `Pushed 0 commits` with two `Write Untitled file`
entries: the automaton emitted `write_file` calls with no `path`, the
DoD gate accepted `task_completed` anyway, and the UI showed a phantom
commit that never landed. This wires up five guardrails so that
scenario now fails loudly and retries on a real path.

Server:
- Reject any `write_file`/`edit_file` tool event with an empty or
  missing `path` as the top-priority DoD check; harden
  `extract_files_changed` to drop empty-path summaries.
- Preflight the local workspace before starting the dev loop: require
  an existing directory, tolerate empty / non-git dirs only when
  `git_repo_url` is configured (first-run clone), reject everything
  else with a remediation hint.
- Emit `git_commit_rolled_back` when the completion gate fails a task
  after a commit SHA was recorded, and persist it via
  `summarise_event_type_for_log`.

Prompt / templates:
- Add `dev_loop_executor_dod_prompt()` in `aura-os-agent-templates`
  spelling out the required `cargo build/test/fmt/clippy` (and
  `pnpm build/test`) commands, forbidding empty-path writes, and
  forbidding no-op `task_completed` calls. Asserted in template tests.

Interface:
- Extend `GitStep` with `commit_rolled_back`, render it with a
  `RotateCcw` icon and strike-through styling, and surface it in the
  log stream labels / summaries.

Tests:
- 12 new replay-style regressions in
  `tests/dev_loop_dod_regression.rs` covering every scenario above.

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): classify 5xx + stream-terminated errors as transient
rawCommits.body:
Extends `classify_infra_failure` with a `ProviderInternalError` class that
matches HTTP 500/502/503/504, their prose forms (Internal Server Error, Bad
Gateway, etc.), and streaming aborts (`stream terminated`, `connection
reset`, `broken pipe`). Previously these fell through to `None` and
failed the task terminally, even though the dev loop's `restart_budget` is
unlimited on this path.

Base cooldown is 10s (shorter than 429's 60s) since 5xx blips typically
clear in seconds. Rate-limit markers still win over 5xx if both appear in
the same reason string, preserving the existing `Retry-After` hint path.

Unblocks automation tasks that hit LLM stream-terminator errors like
`LLM error: stream terminated with error: Internal server error`.

rawCommits.subject: feat(ui): surface task failure + retry-interrupt context in the Run panel
rawCommits.body:
Three related UX fixes for the sidekick Run pane during transient retry
storms, so users can tell apart an abandoned tool call, a completion-gate
rejection, and a real crash.

1. Resolve abandoned pending tool cards on `task_retrying`. Adds
   `resolveAbandonedPendingToolCalls` to `stream/handlers.ts` and
   subscribes to `TaskRetrying` in the task stream bootstrap. When the
   dev loop restarts an automaton after a transient 5xx, every stale
   `write_file` / `edit_file` card flips to "Interrupted by upstream
   error: <reason>" instead of staying stuck on "Writing code...".
2. Show an action-specific title on errored file cards (`Write failed`,
   `Edit failed`, etc.) instead of the generic "Untitled file" shell
   when the tool block never produced a path.
3. Surface the task's `failureReason` on the panel entry. Populated
   live from `task_failed` (with `reason` / `error` / `message`
   fallbacks) and on reload from `tasks.execution_notes` via
   `reconcileStatuses`. Also appends a red completion-gate-rejected
   card when `task_completion_gate` fires with `passed: false`.

Adds `TaskCompletionGate` to both the `EVENT_LABELS` map and the log
stream's `ALL_ENGINE_EVENT_TYPES` so the timeline picks it up, plus
covering tests for retry-interrupt resolution, gate rejection card, and
reason extraction fallbacks.

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): escalate + jitter project cooldowns to tame retry storms
rawCommits.body:
Two improvements to the project cooldown path so a flapping provider
doesn't lock a project into the same window forever:

1. Per-class consecutive-failure escalation. Each repeated hit of the
   same `InfraFailureClass` doubles the base cooldown (1x, 2x, 4x, 8x)
   up to `ESCALATION_CAP`, then flattens. A different class resets the
   counter — classes are treated as independent problems, so three
   5xx's don't punish a subsequent git timeout. `clear_project_cooldown`
   (called after forward progress) drops the entry entirely, which is
   the natural reset path.

2. ±20% jitter on the final cooldown. De-correlates retries across a
   fleet of loops that would otherwise lock-step on the same backoff
   windows after a global provider blip. Uses the clock's subsecond
   nanos as a pseudo-random source to avoid pulling in `rand`. Never
   jitters a non-zero base down to zero, and the cap is enforced
   post-jitter so no sample ever exceeds `PROVIDER_BACKOFF_MAX_SECS`.

New tests cover jitter bounds (256-sample sweep), zero/tiny-base edge
cases, `escalate` multiplier cap, the escalation path through
`register_project_cooldown_with_hint`, and the class-change reset. The
existing `project_cooldown_hint_raises_but_never_lowers_default` test
was updated to use fresh `ProjectId`s per subtest (so the consecutive
counter doesn't leak across cases) and tolerate jitter with range checks.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: escalate + jitter project cooldowns to tame retry storms

rawCommits.subject: feat(reconciler): add pure recovery-action decision engine
rawCommits.body:
Introduces a `reconciler` module that, given a task's persisted
TaskSyncState / TaskRecoveryPoint plus a small amount of execution
context (retry count, failure classification, live-automaton flag,
auto-decompose flag), returns the durable next action the autonomy
supervisor should take: adopt a live run, retry push, retry task,
decompose, mark terminal, or noop.

The function is deliberately pure so it can be reused by both the
existing request-driven dev loop and the upcoming background
reconciliation worker. A `phase7_test_support::reconcile_decision`
helper exposes it for integration tests without widening module
visibility.

First slice of the autonomy plan: stabilizes the checkpoint contract
(reconciler consumes the same TaskSyncState + TaskRecoveryPoint shapes
the task-output API already ships) and lays the groundwork for
push-recovery and decomposition reconciliation before splitting
dev_loop.rs into dedicated services.

Covered by 15 new unit tests in `reconciler::tests` covering each
branch of the decision table.

rawCommits.subject: Axis 4: emit debug.retry_miss telemetry and surface it in heuristics
rawCommits.body:
When `classify_infra_failure` returns `None` but the task-failed
reason still reads like a transient provider/network blip, the dev
loop now emits a `debug.retry_miss` loop-log event tagged with the
reason and the `looks_like_unclassified_transient` hint. This is an
observability signal only — the task still fails terminally — so
each new gap in the classifier becomes visible without silently
retrying.

A matching `unclassified_retry_miss` rule in `aura-run-heuristics`
buckets those events by task id: a single miss gets a `Warn`, three
or more misses on the same task become an `Error`. The analyzer
wires the rule into `analyze` and new integration tests in
`autonomous_recovery_replay.rs` pin both the classifier surface
(`is_provider_internal_error`) and the retry-miss trigger surface
(`looks_like_unclassified_transient`) so regressions are caught at
the integration boundary instead of only in unit tests.

Also exposes two new helpers via `phase7_test_support`:
`is_provider_internal_error` and `looks_like_unclassified_transient`.

rawCommits.subject: Axis 5: stateful resume preamble on retried tasks
rawCommits.body:
When the dev loop restarts a task via `restart_with_infra_backoff`
after a transient failure (Axis 1/2/3 all feed this path), the
harness today boots a fresh automaton with no signal that this is
attempt N. The LLM cheerfully redoes any setup/search/plan work it
had already done on the previous turn and lands back in the same
context shape that triggered the failure in the first place.

This commit adds two pieces of the retry state machine so the next
turn can actually pick up where it left off:

1. An in-memory retry-attempt counter keyed by task id, parallel to
   the existing `remediation_retry_counts` but independent (infra
   retries are a provider/network signal, not a Phase 3 shape
   decision). `bump_retry_attempt` returns 1 on the first restart
   and monotonically increases; `clear_retry_attempt` runs on every
   terminal outcome (`task_completed` and the unrecoverable
   `task_failed` branch) so a future manual re-run starts from 1
   instead of inheriting a stale count.

2. A `build_retry_preamble(attempt, previous_reason)` helper that
   produces a fixed-shape `[aura-retry attempt=N] Previous attempt
   failed with: <trimmed>. Continue from where you left off; avoid
   re-running work that already succeeded.` string. Newlines are
   collapsed and long reasons are truncated to 240 chars + `…` so
   the preamble can't balloon the next prompt even when the failure
   reason carries a 12KB provider body dump.

`try_restart_automaton` now bumps the counter and embeds both
`attempt` and `preamble` on the `task_retrying` domain event. The
interface type (`AuraEventOfType<TaskRetrying>`) is widened
accordingly — `preamble` stays optional on the wire so older
servers still parse cleanly. Five unit tests lock down the
counter/clear pair, the attempt number formatting, the truncation
behavior, the control-char collapse, and the 0 → 1 clamp so UI
parsers can rely on a stable shape.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: Axis 5: stateful resume preamble on retried tasks

rawCommits.subject: feat(reconciler): surface recommended recovery action on task output API
rawCommits.body:
Wires the pure reconciler decision engine into the task-output read
path so every call to `GET /api/projects/:pid/tasks/:tid/output`
includes a `recommended_action` field describing what the recovery
reconciler would do with the task given only persisted state.

Changes
- `reconciler.rs`: add `ReconcileAction::to_json()` and
  `TerminalReason::as_label()` for a stable, single-source wire shape.
- `lib.rs`: `phase7_test_support::reconcile_decision` now delegates to
  `ReconcileAction::to_json`, deduping the JSON conversion.
- `handlers/dev_loop.rs`: new `classify_failure_for_reconciler`
  adapter maps the existing truncation / rate-limited / other reason
  classifier onto the reconciler's `FailureClass`.
- `handlers/tasks.rs`: `TaskOutputResponse` gains an optional
  `recommended_action`. Computed in all three construction paths
  (events, in-memory cache, unavailable fallback). The cache branch
  flags `has_live_automaton = true` so active runs recommend
  `adopt_run` over restart, matching dev_loop's existing policy.
- `interface/src/api/tasks.ts`: union type for the new field so the UI
  can render "retrying push", "decomposing", "provider throttling",
  etc. without guessing the shape.

Advisory only: the backend does not act on the recommendation yet.
The next slice will wire a background worker that reads the same
inputs and dispatches the action through TaskService, AutomatonClient,
and the decomposition pipeline.

Tests
- 4 new `handlers::tasks::tests`:
  - pending_push sync state → `retry_push` with commit_sha,
  - legacy git_push_failed steps → `retry_push`,
  - truncation-shaped `task_failed` reason → `decompose`,
  - rate-limited failure → `mark_terminal` with `rate_limited` reason,

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): raise single-task retry budget to survive one transient provider 500
rawCommits.body:
Previously `run_single_task` pinned the forwarder's restart_budget to
Some(1). That meant a single mid-stream provider / proxy 500 —
correctly classified by `classify_infra_failure` as
`ProviderInternalError` — terminally failed a user-initiated Retry
click, even though the full dev loop (with `restart_budget: None`)
would have automatically recovered from the same error.

Extract the budget into a named constant `SINGLE_TASK_RESTART_BUDGET`
set to 3, which pairs with the existing 10s-base cooldown escalation
to give roughly a 30-60s healing window per retry click without
risking runaway work. Add a regression unit test asserting the budget
is >= 2 and that `take_retry_context` honours it.

Complements the aura-harness commit 913f5cd (Axis 3): together,
mid-stream SSE errors now flow through the reasoner's non-streaming
retry fallback AND, if they still surface, the server gives the
automaton enough restarts to absorb a single provider blip.

Previously `cargo run -p aura-os-desktop -- --external-harness` did
nothing: the desktop binary didn't parse any argv, so the flag was

helper that requires `LOCAL_HARNESS_URL` to be set, probes `/health` up
front, sets `AURA_DESKTOP_EXTERNAL_HARNESS=1` so the runtime config
surfaces the mode correctly, and fails fast (exit 2) with an actionable
message if the env is missing or the harness isn't reachable. When the
flag is set, the bundled-sidecar autospawn path is skipped entirely so
it can't stomp on the external URL.

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): route completion-gate failures through infra retry ladder
rawCommits.body:
The `task_completed` completion-validation gate was terminating tasks
whose underlying failure was a transient provider 5xx (e.g. `LLM error:
stream terminated with error: Internal server error`). The classifier
correctly tagged the error as `ProviderInternalError`, but the gate's
own synthesised reason ("no output, no file changes, no verification
evidence") never classified as infra-transient, so the retry ladder in
`task_failed` was bypassed entirely.

Capture a `last_transient_reason` breadcrumb whenever a preceding
`error` event classifies as an infra transient, and route the
completion-gate failure branch through a shared `attempt_infra_retry`
helper using that breadcrumb (falling back to the gate reason). On a
`Retried` outcome we swap the broadcast receiver and continue the
event loop instead of emitting rollback/persisting failure metadata;
on `RetryFailed { task_reset_to_ready }` we bridge
`ready -> in_progress` before the terminal `failed` transition so
aura-storage doesn't reject the direct `ready -> failed`.

Regression pinned by a unit test in `handlers::dev_loop::tests` and an
integration test in `autonomous_recovery_replay.rs` asserting the
gate-only reason is non-infra while the tracked breadcrumb is a
`ProviderInternalError` -- together they guarantee the breadcrumb
flips the retry path on whenever both are available.

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): make empty-path write recoverable when a real write lands
rawCommits.body:
Task 2.4 (`Identity::create`) regressed the Definition-of-Done gate:
the harness emitted a handful of `write_file` tool calls with an
empty `path` argument, surfaced the mistake to the agent inline, and
the agent recovered by re-issuing the call with a real path. The
real-path write landed on disk and all four verification gates
(build/test/fmt/lint) passed against it. The DoD gate still rejected
the `task_done` because `empty_path_writes > 0`, leaving the task in
a limbo state (commit locally, task not marked done) despite the
actual work being correct.

The empty-path check now only fails when the agent never recovered
-- i.e. when `files_changed` is empty. A run that produced at least
one real file change is evidence that the misfire was transient, and
the verification-step checks already enforce DoD on the real writes,
so there is no safety loss from letting the run through. An
unrecovered misfire (empty-path writes with zero files_changed) is
still the unambiguous rejection case and continues to fail the gate
so the dev loop retries.

Tests in `dev_loop_dod_regression` are split to cover both the
rejection-on-unrecovered-misfire path and the acceptance-on-recovery
path; unit tests under `handlers::dev_loop` mirror the same
contract. The public `CachedTaskOutput.empty_path_writes` doc string
and the test-only shim in `lib.rs` are updated so downstream
consumers understand the new semantics.

rawCommits.body:

push_failed, or any is_push-classified task_failed) no longer demote
a task from done to failed. Task transitions are determined only by
completion_validation_failure_reason; push is best-effort.

Introduces a new push_deferred domain event carrying the commit sha
and the reason, plus a per-project consecutive_push_failures counter
that emits a one-shot project_push_stuck event once the streak hits
CONSECUTIVE_PUSH_FAILURES_STUCK_THRESHOLD = 3. The counter resets on
any successful push.

Unblocks projects like the current main branch where the remote is
out of disk: tasks now complete cleanly, the UI surfaces the push
backlog, and the loop keeps running other tasks.

Adds regression cases in dev_loop_dod_regression.rs for gate-pass +
push-fail to done across all three push failure classes.

rawCommits.subject: feat(orbit): guard against ENOSPC with cooldown + UI remediation
rawCommits.body:
When orbit's rootfs fills up, `git push` fails with `No space left on
device` and retries just pile on more partial packs. Aura now:

- Classifies this as `remote_storage_exhausted` and trips a new
  `OrbitCapacityGuard` (per `ORBIT_BASE_URL`, 15-minute default cooldown,
  tunable via `AURA_ORBIT_ENOSPC_COOLDOWN_SECS`).
- Enriches `push_deferred` / `project_push_stuck` events with
  `remediation` + `retry_after_secs`, and promotes storage-exhausted
  failures to a `project_push_stuck` banner on the first hit instead of
  waiting for a streak.
- Clears the guard on the next successful `git_pushed` event.
- Adds `OrbitClient::health()` + cached `discovery()` for future
  pre-flight / introspection needs.
- Surfaces the state in the UI: amber "degraded" dot on the Orbit
  indicator with an "Orbit out of disk" row, and a class-specific
  label on the GitStepItem showing the retry window.
- Documents the ENOSPC runbook (diagnosis, cleanup steps, cooldown
  knob) in `docs/render-deployment.md`.

Covered by new tests in `event-store.test.tsx`, the orbit discovery
deserialization tests, and a regression test pinning the real-world
orbit ENOSPC reason string to `RemoteStorageExhausted`.

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): per-tool-call retry budget consuming tool_call_failed
rawCommits.body:
aura-harness recently grew an internal streaming-retry-with-backoff
loop (commit 9174501 in aura-harness) that emits AutomatonEvent
tool_call_retrying / tool_call_failed when a transient LLM provider
error breaks mid-tool-use. Before this change the server just
mirrored tool_call_failed out to the UI and relied on the task-level
SINGLE_TASK_RESTART_BUDGET to catch the follow-up task_failed; but
that is a different ladder with coarser semantics and doesn't
recover a tool-level stream abort until the task itself fails.

Add a dedicated server-side retry budget that lives alongside the
existing task-level retry machinery:

- TOOL_CALL_RETRY_BUDGET = 8, matching aura-harness's internal
  AURA_LLM_MAX_RETRIES so the two ladders agree on the ceiling and
  the UI can confidently render "retried N/8".
- Per-task HashMap<task_id, u32> counter scoped to the forwarder
  loop; bumped only when the infra-transient classifier accepts the
  harness's reason string (overloaded_error / 5xx / stream
  terminated / rate limit / timeout hints).
- Retry dispatch runs outside any cache-lock critical section; a
  `pending_tool_call_retry` marker is staged inside the cache arm
  and handled after the event is broadcast to the UI, so the UI
  always sees the failure badge before the automaton restarts.
- attempt_infra_retry is reused verbatim -- the same code path that
  handles task-level infra retries now handles tool-call-level ones,
  swapping the rx stream and resetting terminal_seen / session_status
  so the loop resumes cleanly on the new automaton.

Non-transient reasons never retry. Budget exhaustion falls through
to the existing task-level path, which then applies its own
SINGLE_TASK_RESTART_BUDGET ceiling -- the two budgets compose
multiplicatively, which is intentional and matches how users
experience a truly unrecoverable upstream.

Expose two helpers via phase7_test_support (tsp::) so the
classification + budget logic is reachable from
tests/dev_loop_dod_regression.rs without standing up the whole
forwarder:

- tool_call_failed_should_retry(reason, prior_count) -> bool
- tool_call_retry_budget() -> u32 (compile-time constant)

Regression tests cover:

- Budget is exactly 8 and matches the harness retry count.
- provider_internal_error / rate_limit / push_timeout reasons are
  retry-eligible while under budget.
- Budget exhaustion stops retries even for transient reasons.
- Non-transient reasons are never retried.

No changes to SINGLE_TASK_RESTART_BUDGET semantics and no changes to
the task-terminal-status invariant from 73ab86fb: task done/failed
transitions still key off completion_validation_failure_reason only.
rawCommits.cleanSubject: per-tool-call retry budget consuming tool_call_failed

rawCommits.subject: feat(interface): live retry status on tool cards from aura-harness retry events
rawCommits.body:
Wire the new ToolCallRetrying / ToolCallFailed events that
aura-harness emits during its streaming-retry-with-backoff loop
(commit 9174501 in aura-harness) through to the tool-card renderer
so users see a live "Writing retrying (n/8)..." state instead of
the stale "Writing code..." header that looked stuck during
upstream 5xx storms.

Changes:

- aura-events.ts: add EventType.ToolCallRetrying /
  EventType.ToolCallFailed enum members and matching AuraEvent
  discriminated-union content shapes (attempt/max_attempts/delay_ms/
  reason for retrying; reason for failed). Doc-links each variant
  to the harness event that produces it and the server retry
  ladder (TOOL_CALL_RETRY_BUDGET) that precedes it.
- stream.ts: extend ToolCallEntry with retrying / retryAttempt /
  retryMax / retryReason / retryExhausted fields. Fields are
  latched across snapshot merges -- a fresh ToolCallSnapshot clears
  `retrying` (bytes flowing again) but preserves attempt/max/reason
  so a later failure can still report "retried N/max".
- api/streams.ts: add ToolCallRetryingInfo / ToolCallFailedInfo
  info types that match the reducer shape.
- hooks/stream/handlers.ts: add handleToolCallRetrying (flip entry
  into retrying state, synthesize a pending placeholder when the
  start event hasn't arrived yet), handleToolCallFailed (latch
  retryExhausted, mark errored, synthesize result from reason,
  propagate into already-saved events the same way
  resolveToolCallInEvents does). handleToolCallSnapshot and
  handleToolResult now clear `retrying` on success. Added unit
  tests covering placeholder synthesis, snapshot-clears-retrying
  while preserving attempt/max, failure latches retryExhausted,
  saved-events propagation, and empty-reason fallback.
- stores/task-stream-bootstrap.ts: subscribe the two new EventTypes
  and route them into the per-task refs/setters via the new
  reducers. Tolerates out-of-order delivery (retrying can arrive
  before started because the WS + SSE pipeline can reorder under
  load).
- components/Block/renderers/FileBlock.tsx: drop the opportunistic
  input.retry_attempt / input.retry_max stub and read directly from
  the new ToolCallEntry fields. Live title reads "Writing retrying
  (n/max)..." while `retrying` is true; on a final failure with
  exhausted retries the header gains a "retried n/max" prefix so
  users see the full recovery history, not just the last stream
  abort.
- hooks/use-log-stream-labels.ts: add EVENT_LABELS entries for the
  two new EventTypes (labeled "Tool") so the Record<EventType,string>
  contract stays exhaustive and tsc -b keeps passing.

No backend changes in this commit; the server-side retry budget
that consumes tool_call_failed landed separately so the typecheck
+ vitest runs are independently bisectable.

rawCommits.subject: feat(desktop): fail fast when --external-harness has run_command disabled
rawCommits.body:
Closes the 3.0-class run_command regression for operator-managed
external harnesses. The previous fix made the desktop-spawned
aura-node sidecar honour AURA_AUTONOMOUS_DEV_LOOP, but when the
operator runs the harness themselves (--external-harness +
LOCAL_HARNESS_URL), the desktop never controls its env and a forgotten
AURA_AUTONOMOUS_DEV_LOOP=1 silently re-introduces the category-gate
denial that shows up as 20-second tool-callback timeouts.

enforce_external_harness_or_exit now fetches the harness's new
/health policy fields (landed in aura-harness commit 32c0856) via a
raw-TCP probe_http_get_json helper, classifies the result with a pure
classify_external_harness_policy function, and:

  - run_command_enabled=true   -> proceed (info log).
  - run_command_enabled=false  -> exit(2) with a diagnostic that names
                                  the exact envs the operator needs.
  - field missing / non-bool   -> warn and proceed (pre-policy-
                                  disclosure aura-node builds).
  - unparseable body           -> warn and proceed.

Unit tests pin all four branches of the decision matrix. The classifier
is split out as a pure function so the tests can run without faking
std::process::exit or spinning up a real harness.

rawCommits.subject: feat(agent-tools): ship every capable tool by default
rawCommits.body:
The session assembler had two independent gates: a capability check
and a Surface::OnDemand check that required the LLM to promote a
tool's ToolDomain via load_domain_tools before it would ship. No
server caller ever fed a non-empty loaded_domains slice back into
build_session_tools, so domain-scoped tools (list_specs, get_spec,
etc.) were structurally un-callable even when the agent held the
right capabilities -- the LLM would loop calling load_domain_tools
and retrying the real tool only to be told it "is not allowed".

Drop the Surface filter from build_session_tool_names so every tool
whose required_capabilities() the agent satisfies now ships in the
default session payload. load_domain_tools is kept as a no-op for
backwards compatibility with older system prompts. Capability-based
security is untouched -- project-scoped tools still require the
matching ReadProject/WriteProject grant.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): auto-splice project self-caps on project-bound chat open
rawCommits.body:
Non-CEO agents with an empty `capabilities` bundle were hitting
`Tool 'X' is not allowed` from the harness kernel for every
project-scoped tool (`get_project`, `list_specs`, `create_spec`,
`create_task`, `run_task`, ...). The manifest filter in
`build_cross_agent_tools` dropped those tools via
`permissions_satisfy_requirements`, and the kernel's fail-closed
`allow_unlisted = false` default then denied each call by name.

Introduce `AgentPermissions::with_project_self_caps(project_id)`
that splices `ReadProject` + `WriteProject` grants for the bound
project (no-op if a matching exact grant or a
`ReadAllProjects`/`WriteAllProjects` wildcard is already held).

Wire it into both chat entry points:

- `agent_instance_chat`: unconditional splice (instances are always
  project-bound), and ship the spliced bundle as the harness's
  `agent_permissions` too so the manifest filter and the per-call
  `holds_capability` check see the same grants.
- `agent_chat`: splice gated on `body.project_id` being present so
  non-project chats keep their narrower bundle.

Not a privilege escalation: the splice only grants self-project
access, and the downstream aura-storage / aura-network handlers
re-verify project membership via the session JWT on every real API
call. The cap gate was a redundant first-pass filter for the one
project the session is bound to.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: auto-splice project self-caps on project-bound chat open

rawCommits.subject: fix(agents): fall back to persist_ctx project_id for self-caps splice
rawCommits.body:
The previous fix only spliced project self-caps when the chat body
carried `project_id`. The interface's `streamAgentEvents` helper
only forwards that field when the caller scopes the chat to a
project explicitly (e.g. from a project view). Chats initiated
from the bare agents panel — or from any client that trusts the
persisted project binding — left `body.project_id` as `None` even
though the agent was already bound to a project via the
`project_agents` record materialised into `persist_ctx.project_id`.

Result: the harness kept denying `list_specs` / `create_spec` /
`get_project` etc. with `"Tool 'X' is not allowed"` because the
manifest filter ran against the raw (empty-caps) bundle.

Use `body.project_id` first, falling back to `persist_ctx.project_id`
when the body omits it. The persistence context is already resolved
upstream by `setup_agent_chat_persistence_with_matched` so we're
only threading an existing value into the splice, not paying a new
round-trip.

This does not change behaviour for `agent_instance_chat` (which was
already unconditional via the path-bound `project_id`) or for
non-project agent chats (still a no-op when neither the body nor
the persistence context carries an id).

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: fall back to persist_ctx project_id for self-caps splice

rawCommits.subject: fix(agent-tools): inject session project_id on cross-agent dispatch
rawCommits.body:
Project-scoped tools (`create_spec`, `create_task`, `list_specs`,
...) historically required the LLM to thread `project_id` through
every call even when the chat session is bound to exactly one
project. When the model forgot, the call failed at `require_str`
in the tool's `execute` method even though the server had all the
context it needed to resolve the id deterministically.

Stamp `X-Aura-Project-Id` on every cross-agent `InstalledTool` the
harness receives, mirroring the existing `X-Aura-Agent-Id` /
`X-Aura-Org-Id` stamps. The `/api/agent_tools/:name` dispatcher
now reads the header and injects the value into the JSON args
whenever the model omits `project_id`, before the capability
check runs. This makes `CapabilityRequirement::WriteProjectFromArg`
pass deterministically for project-bound sessions and matches the
session_project_id fallback the harness's `DomainToolExecutor`
already performs on the direct-`DomainApi` dispatch path.

rawCommits.subject: refactor(agent-tools): drop project_id from required schemas
rawCommits.body:
The aura-os-server cross-agent dispatcher now injects project_id
from the X-Aura-Project-Id header before the capability check
runs, so project-scoped tools no longer need the LLM to thread
it through every call. Remove `project_id` from the `required`
array of each project-scoped tool's parameters_schema while
keeping it in `properties` so the LLM can still pass it
explicitly for cross-project calls (CEO preset).

rawCommits.subject: test(agent-tools): pin project_id header-injection behavior
rawCommits.body:
Add regression coverage for the def464a6 / 13acee22 fix that
lets project-scoped tools work without the LLM threading
project_id through every call on a project-bound chat
session. Without these tests a future refactor of
dispatch_agent_tool or stamp_agent_tool_auth could silently
re-break create_spec / create_task on every project-
instance chat.

Covers:
- dispatch_agent_tool injects `project_id` from
  X-Aura-Project-Id header when args omit it (promoting a
  `Value::Null` body to an object if necessary, and ignoring
  whitespace-only header values)
- args-side `project_id` wins over header when both present
  (header is a fallback, not an override — preserves
  cross-project invocations from a project-bound session)
- no-op when neither header nor args carry project_id, so
  the downstream capability check still fails the way it
  always has
- `project_id` stays absent from CreateSpecTool's `required`
  schema array (stays in `properties`)

Implementation notes:
- Extracted the inline injection block in
  `dispatch_agent_tool` into a testable
  `inject_project_id_from_header` helper at module scope so
  the four branches above can be unit-tested directly
  without the router / storage / harness. Behaviour is
  byte-identical to the previous inlined version.
- Integration-level coverage via apps/aura-os-server/tests/
  was not pursued because the shared test harness in
  tests/common/mod.rs currently fails to build on main
  (missing `orbit_capacity_guard` field on AppState); fixing
  that is out of scope for this change.

rawCommits.body:
The frameless tao window had no NSMenu attached, so AppKit silently
swallowed standard edit-shortcut key equivalents instead of dispatching
them up the responder chain to the WebView. Right-click Paste worked
because the WebView's own context menu calls the Cocoa selectors
directly, but Cmd+C / Cmd+V in input fields (e.g. the login screen)
did nothing.

and edit submenus. The edit items are `PredefinedMenuItem`s wired to
the canonical Cocoa selectors (`copy:`, `paste:`, `cut:`, `selectAll:`,
`undo:`, `redo:`), which the WebView (first responder) handles
natively. No-op on Windows/Linux where WebView2/GTK already handle
these shortcuts in input controls without an app menu.

rawCommits.body:
WKWebView (macOS) and WebKitGTK (Linux) ignore `-webkit-app-region:
drag` -- it's a Chromium-only extension, never upstreamed to WebKit
proper -- so the frameless titlebar had no drag behavior on Apple
Silicon. Route pointerdown inside `.titlebar-drag` regions through the
existing `WinCmd::Drag` IPC, which `tao::Window::drag_window()` handles
natively on every platform. Scoped off Windows so WebView2 keeps using
the native CSS path.

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): preserve mid-turn state across page refresh
rawCommits.body:
When refreshing while an assistant turn was streaming, the chat lost
all in-flight text/tool cards and the sidekick lost its pending
spec/task placeholders until the turn finally ended. Recover the full
turn from the events the server is already persisting:

- Add an `in_flight` flag on `SessionEvent` and synthesize a partial
  assistant turn in `events_to_session_history` from

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): bridge ready->failed and make retry idempotent
rawCommits.body:
Two server-side interactions with aura-storage's task state machine
were rejecting legal-intent requests:

- persist_task_failure_reason (called from run_single_task's
  connect-failure path and synthesize_task_failed) issued a direct
  ready -> failed transition, which aura-storage rejects (only
  in_progress -> failed is legal). The other two terminal-failure
  sites in dev_loop already bridge via in_progress; port the same
  pattern here, and skip the write entirely when the task is already
  failed/done so repeated events don't re-log the warning.

  when the infra-retry ladder had already reset a task to ready. Make
  the handler idempotent on ready: return the current task unchanged
  instead of hitting validate_transition. Failed/blocked -> ready
  paths are unchanged.

Adds a unit test pinning the ready->ready invariant so the new
short-circuit stays in place, and leans on the existing
ready_to_failed_requires_bridging_via_in_progress test for the
dev-loop bridge.

rawCommits.subject: fix(tasks): unify state machine with storage via safe_transition
rawCommits.body:
The dev loop's 400 "Invalid status transition" spew and retry-400s
traced back to aura-os-server running a looser task state machine
than the one aura-storage actually enforces. TaskService accepted
`(InProgress, Ready)` and `(Failed, InProgress)` but storage then
rejected both at the HTTP boundary, and dev_loop.rs worked around
that with a scatter of conditional hand-rolled bridges that only
fired on specific flags.

Centralise every transition aura-os-server makes through one
bridge-aware helper:

- crates/aura-os-tasks/src/transition.rs: new `safe_transition`
  helper plus `compute_bridge` planner. compute_bridge pins the
  storage-legal edge list as a const and is exercised by a full
  (from,to) matrix test that asserts every hop returned is a
  storage-legal edge and the bridge ends at the target.
- crates/aura-os-tasks/src/task_service.rs: trim
  `validate_transition` to exactly the storage-legal edge list
  (plus the aura-os-only Backlog/ToDo edges the planner uses
  before anything hits storage), with a source-of-truth comment
  pointing at aura-storage/repo.rs. `reset_task_to_ready`,
  `reset_in_progress_tasks`, and `retry_task` now delegate to
  safe_transition.
- apps/aura-os-server/src/handlers/dev_loop.rs: replace the five
  direct `storage_client.transition_task` call sites (completion-
  gate failure, completion-gate done, push-failure done,
  infra-retry-ladder failure, connect-failure failure) with
  `safe_transition`, deleting the conditional `ready -> in_progress`
  bridges that would have missed any state the flags didn't
  enumerate.
- apps/aura-os-server/src/handlers/tasks.rs: replace the hand-
  rolled idempotent-on-ready short-circuit in `retry_task` with a
  single `safe_transition(Ready)` call. The InProgress retry
  case (which used to 400 at storage because TaskService had said
  OK) now correctly bridges via Failed.

Observability: also surface the harness's detail at warn! level
so the "automaton died in 90ms with no visible reason" class of
bug is diagnosable from os-server logs alone:

- `Error` events now warn with the harness's reason string before
  the retry/synthesise ladder runs.
- `Done`-without-terminal warns with the synthesis reason.
- Stream-closed-without-terminal warns pointing at the harness
  stdout for the `tick failed` / `on_install failed` root cause.

Tests:

- 7 new transition-matrix tests (idempotence, direct edges,
  multi-hop correctness, terminal-Done, no-target-Pending,
  Backlog/ToDo rejection, the four regression edges that 400'd).
- `non_storage_edges_are_rejected_by_direct_validator` pins the
  two edges that moved to the bridging helper.
- `retry_bridge_planner_contract` replaces the outdated
  `retry_on_already_ready_task_must_short_circuit` test with one
  that exercises the actual planner the handler hangs on.
- Existing `valid_transitions_succeed` / `reset_from_in_progress`
  tests updated to reflect the new storage-aligned list.

`completion_validation_failure_reason` rejected finished tasks with

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): close harness WS on restart to stop exhausting 128-slot cap
rawCommits.body:
`AutomatonClient::connect_event_stream` used to spawn a detached reader
task and return only the broadcast `Sender`, with no handle for the
caller to close the underlying WebSocket. Every infra retry, adopt-on-
conflict, and stop-loop therefore opened a fresh stream but left the
previous reader and its harness `ws_slots` permit alive, so the
harness-side semaphore (capped at 128 in `aura-node`) filled up and
every subsequent `/stream/automaton/:id` upgrade returned
`503 Service Unavailable` with `WS connection cap reached`.

Introduce a `WsReaderHandle` (RAII wrapper around the reader's
`AbortHandle`) and thread it from `connect_event_stream` through
`runner::connect_with_retries` / `runner::start_and_connect` into the
`dev_loop` forwarder. The forwarder owns the handle for its task
lifetime and replaces it on every `InfraRetryOutcome::Retried` /
direct `restart_with_infra_backoff` swap, so the old socket closes
before the new one takes its place. `Drop` aborts as a safety net, so
any unexpected exit (stop-loop abort, panic, early return) also frees
the slot.

Covered by three `tokio` tests in `automaton_client` that assert
`cancel()`, `Drop`, and the `Arc`-clone semantics (clones keep the
reader alive until the last one drops).

rawCommits.subject: feat(dev-loop): add DoD retry tier with language-neutral follow-up prompt
rawCommits.body:
The Definition-of-Done completion gate previously had a single recovery
ladder (attempt_infra_retry) for provider 5xx / rate limits. A DoD
rejection caused by the agent forgetting to run `cargo build` / `cargo
test` / `cargo fmt` / `cargo clippy` fell straight through to terminal
failure, even though the fix was usually one re-prompt away.

Adds an attempt_dod_retry tier that sits between the infra retry and
the terminal-failure path:

  * classify_dod_remediation_kind maps the gate's prose reason to a
    DoDRemediationKind (MissingBuild | MissingTest | MissingFmt |
    MissingLint), or None for structural failures (empty-path writes,
    baseline no-activity, kernel-policy denials) that cannot be fixed
    by another turn.
  * MAX_DOD_RETRIES_PER_TASK caps the tier at 2 attempts per task so a
    fundamentally-stuck agent can't burn tokens forever; counters live
    in-memory and reset on terminal success/failure.
  * build_dod_followup_prompt emits a stable, language-neutral note
    ("[aura-dod-retry attempt=N axis=...]") that echoes the gate's own
    reason verbatim. Earlier iterations hard-coded cargo commands;
    that lies to the agent on TypeScript / Python / mixed-language
    tasks, so the prompt now names the missing axis in prose and lets
    the gate reason (which already says "cargo build or equivalent
    must pass") carry the concrete-command hint.
  * attempt_dod_retry persists the note to execution_notes, emits a
    task_dod_retry domain event with {task_id, reason, attempt,
    remediation_kind}, resets the task to ready, and calls
    restart_with_infra_backoff.

Rewires the task_completed gate-failure block to call attempt_dod_retry
after attempt_infra_retry returns NotClassified. On a successful DoD
retry the commit rollback event is suppressed because the next turn's
commit will replace the current one; on budget exhaustion or a
non-remediable reason the path falls through to terminal failure
exactly as before.

Regression coverage in tests/dev_loop_dod_regression.rs pins the
classifier mappings, the prompt's passthrough shape, the axis-in-prose
contract, the no-cargo-hard-coding invariant, the truncation budget,
and the retry budget constant.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): extend kernel-policy-denial gate upgrade to all DoD axes
rawCommits.body:
Task 1.0 "Initialise Rust workspace" (409fa99a-274b-4cea-88ef-23cbc506ce93)
failed after burning both DoD retry attempts against a harness lock-down
that no re-prompt could move. Root cause was two narrow matchers in the
completion gate:

1. The kernel-policy-denial diagnostic only fired inside the !has_build
   branch. When the gate registered any build signal (auto-triggered
   cargo check independent of run_command, in the incident's case), it
   sailed past the upgrade and rejected at !has_test / !has_fmt /
   !has_lint with the generic "no X step was run" message. The DoD
   retry classifier correctly treated that as retryable and burned
   attempts prompting the agent to re-run a tool that was still denied.

2. The denial pattern substring was "is not allowed", which missed the
   newer "'shell_script' requires allow_shell=true (per-call or in
   ToolConfig)" string the harness emitted during the incident.

Extract the denial check into run_command_denied_by_policy() with
broadened patterns (is not allowed / requires allow_shell /
enable_cmd_tools / aura_strict_mode / tool policy, all case-insensitive)
and consult it once up front in completion_validation_failure_reason.
If any of the four axes (!has_build, !has_test, !has_fmt, !has_lint)
would otherwise fail AND a denial is recorded, short-circuit to the
existing RUN_COMMAND_POLICY_DENIED_REASON constant. The DoD-retry
classifier already rejects that reason (dod_classifier_rejects_non_
remediable_reasons), so the retry tier now correctly falls through to
terminal failure with an actionable diagnostic instead of looping.

Five new regressions in dev_loop_dod_regression.rs pin the incident
surface: policy-denial for missing test/fmt/lint axes, recognition of
the requires-allow_shell string verbatim, and end-to-end verification
that the retry classifier returns None when the denial triggers at a
non-build axis.

rawCommits.subject: fix(dev-loop): stop tight-loop reconnect on terminal harness errors
rawCommits.body:
The error-event handler unconditionally restarted the harness automaton
whenever the retry budget was non-zero. Combined with the dev loop's
`restart_budget: None` (unlimited) and the adopt-on-conflict path
reconnecting to a still-running automaton, any terminal `error` event
from the harness drove a tight ~1.3s WS reconnect loop. The harness
would replay its last terminal event to each new subscriber, and we'd
land back in the same branch.

The concrete signal from the incident (task d12b2cc3) was the harness's
own anti-waste guard:

  "CRITICAL: All tool calls have returned errors for 5 consecutive
   iterations. The agent appears stuck. Stopping to prevent waste."

That signal is by construction deterministic from the server's side --
the agent already gave up, and restarting re-runs the same prompt
against the same context.

Gate the error-event restart on whether the reason is actually
transient. New `should_restart_on_error_event(reason)` returns true
iff the reason classifies as infra-transient (or matches the
unclassified-transient heuristic) AND is not a terminal agent-stuck
signal. The anti-waste guard wins over any substring overlap with
transient keywords.

New `is_agent_stuck_terminal_signal(reason)` matches the harness
anti-waste vocabulary (case-insensitive): "appears stuck", "is stuck",
"consecutive iterations/errors/failures", "stopping to prevent waste",
"stopping to conserve", and the "CRITICAL: ... stopping" convention.

Both helpers exposed through `phase7_test_support` so the decision
matrix can be pinned without replaying the full forwarder.

Tests:
- agent_stuck_classifier_recognises_harness_anti_waste_signal: the
  verbatim incident reason plus reasonable variants must all classify
  as terminal.
- agent_stuck_classifier_rejects_normal_errors: routine compile /
  tool / rate-limit reasons must NOT be mis-classified as stuck.
- error_event_gate_matrix_restart_only_on_actual_transients: full
  restart-vs-fail decision matrix covering agent-stuck, classified
  transients, unclassified-transient heuristic, and deterministic
  errors.

Deferred (orthogonal): the "adopt live automaton on restart conflict"
path should also treat an automaton whose last event was a terminal
error as stale and cleanly restart it. Once this gate lands, the
stuck-agent case no longer reaches that code path, so it's a
follow-up rather than a live bug.

Made-with: Cursor

rawCommits.subject: fix: close binary_allowlist policy-denial gap end-to-end
rawCommits.body:
`aura-tools::check_binary_allowlist` was flipped to fail closed when
`enable_commands: true` and `ToolConfig::binary_allowlist` is empty:
every exec is rejected with `forbidden: command execution requires a
non-empty binary_allowlist; configure ToolConfig::binary_allowlist`.
The aura-os side hadn't caught up:

1. The bundled desktop sidecar sets `AURA_ALLOW_SHELL=1` but
   explicitly avoided setting `AURA_ALLOWED_COMMANDS`, on the
   assumption that "empty = all allowed" still held. With the new
   behaviour this guaranteed every DoD verification command
   (`cargo build/test/fmt/clippy`) was denied and no task could ever
   finish when run through the desktop app.
2. The README + evals stack README told external-harness operators
   the same wrong thing, so anyone starting `aura-node` manually hit
   the denial as soon as they tried a real task.
3. The DoD gate's policy-denial classifier matched five other
   denial patterns but not `binary_allowlist`, so the gate emitted
   its generic "no build step was run" diagnostic when this denial
   fired and the DoD retry tier burned attempts on a lockdown that
   another turn cannot possibly fix.

Changes:

- `apps/aura-os-desktop/src/main.rs`: set
  `AURA_ALLOWED_COMMANDS` to a vetted default covering every DoD
  axis (cargo/rustc/rustfmt/cargo-clippy/cargo-fmt/git + common
  shells + node/python toolchains). Operator-provided values in
  the parent env are preserved (the explicit `.env()` call is
  gated on `std::env::var_os("AURA_ALLOWED_COMMANDS").is_none()`).
  Comment rewritten to describe the actual fail-closed behaviour.
- `README.md` + `infra/evals/local-stack/README.md`: describe both
  policy gates (`AURA_ALLOW_SHELL` + `AURA_ALLOWED_COMMANDS`) as
  required, with explicit bash and PowerShell recipes. Flag that
  the "empty allowlist = all allowed" convention has been removed
  for the binary allow-list specifically.
- `apps/aura-os-server/src/handlers/dev_loop.rs`:
  `run_command_denied_by_policy` now matches `binary_allowlist` /
  `binary allowlist` (case-insensitive). `RUN_COMMAND_POLICY_
  DENIED_REASON` is updated to mention the `AURA_ALLOW_SHELL` +
  `AURA_ALLOWED_COMMANDS` knobs so the operator-facing diagnostic
  is actually actionable.
- `apps/aura-os-server/tests/dev_loop_dod_regression.rs`: three
  new regression tests pinning the verbatim denial string from
  `aura-tools::check_binary_allowlist`:
    * `gate_recognises_binary_allowlist_denial_as_policy_denial`
    * `gate_recognises_binary_allowlist_denial_at_test_axis`
    * `dod_classifier_rejects_binary_allowlist_policy_denial`

All 52 tests in `dev_loop_dod_regression` green, `cargo check`
clean on `aura-os-desktop` and `aura-os-server`. No changes to
rustfmt/clippy surface in the code I touched.

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): render historical tool markers as collapsible Blocks
rawCommits.body:
The sidekick task overlay had regressed to rendering [tool: ...] markers as
flat inline rows once a task finished. Live streams already routed markers
through expandToolMarkersInTimeline, but completed tasks (and any other
LLMOutput consumer reading raw assistant text from the turn cache or the
plain-text task output fallback) bypassed it and fell into SegmentedContent's
legacy InlineToolMarker renderer.

Hoist the expansion + synthetic timeline build into LLMOutput so every code
path - streaming, MessageBubble, TaskOutputSection - emits real tool entries
that go through the shared Block registry. LLMStreamOutput is now a thin
phase-indicator wrapper, and SegmentedContent is reduced to a markdown-only
renderer (its inline marker / auto-build splitting and module CSS are gone).

rawCommits.subject: fix(chat): preserve assistant turn at end of stream
rawCommits.body:
The assistant message and any tool calls used to disappear from the
chat view immediately after a turn finished, with the user prompt the
only visible artifact until a hard reload. The persisted state on the
server was always intact -- this was purely client-side rendering loss.

Three independent client-side bugs combined to produce the symptom:

1. `handleAssistantTurnBoundary` only saved a `stream-*` event when the
   buffered text was non-empty, so any "tool calls only" turn was
   silently wiped by the subsequent `resetStreamBuffers`. Save when
   either text OR new (unsnapshotted) tool calls are present.

2. The forced post-stream `fetchHistory({ force: true })` regularly
   raced server-side persistence of `assistant_message_end`, returning
   a partial / sanitized snapshot that the hydrate-to-stream effect
   then used to overwrite freshly-finalized live events. Add a 1500ms
   grace window after a streaming -> not-streaming transition that
   skips the reset, plus a "stream mutated more recently than history
   was persisted" guard for the same class of races outside the
   window.

3. `combineStoredAndStreamMessages` couldn't anchor a leading
   optimistic user bubble at stored[0] when no neighbours had matched
   yet, so a `[user-temp, asst-stream]` stream paired with content-
   mismatched `[user-real, asst-real]` history dropped the user into
   `liveOnlyMessages` and back-walk-matched the assistant content out
   of the view. Allow `index === 0` to anchor when the stream
   represents a multi-row turn (length >= 2), keeping the existing
   protection for lone optimistic bubbles whose content happens to
   repeat earlier history.

Adds unit tests for each fix.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: preserve assistant turn at end of stream

rawCommits.subject: feat(loops): isolate concurrent loops with topic-scoped EventHub + LoopRegistry
rawCommits.body:
Introduces aura-os-events and aura-os-loops crates so chat, automation,
task, process, and spec loops are tracked per (user, project,
agent_instance, agent, kind, instance) instead of fanning out through
one global event broadcast. Producers publish DomainEvents on
topic-scoped channels and loops report progress through a LoopHandle
whose terminal state is gated by an atomic flag so spawned event
handlers can hold Arc<LoopHandle> without leaking partial completions.

Re-keys the in-memory automaton_registry and task_output_cache to
(ProjectId, AgentInstanceId) and (ProjectId, TaskId) to remove the
ambiguous single-key lookups that allowed cross-agent bleed when more
than one loop ran in the same project. Per-project claim mutex stays
serialized for atomicity, but task selection now filters by
agent_instance_id so concurrent claimers see disjoint candidates.

Adds GET /api/loops snapshot, a loop_events_bridge that fans typed
DomainEvents out to the legacy /ws/events broadcast as JSON, and a
client-side useLoopActivityStore that consumes both feeds with a
hydration-on-reconnect step and a 60s stall watchdog. A single
LoopProgress component then renders the resulting activity in the agent
list rows, the project sidekick Tasks/Run tabs, and per-task explorer
rows so the circular indicator stays consistent across UI surfaces.

Replaces SwarmHarness::new() expect with a logged fallback per
rules-rust.md.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: isolate concurrent loops with topic-scoped EventHub + LoopRegistry

rawCommits.subject: fix(ui): hoist live task status into shared zustand store
rawCommits.body:
useTaskStatus mirrored task.execution_notes into local state via a
render-time useEffect; clearing failReason in handleRetry re-seeded
it from the still-present prop, looping the sidekick into "Maximum
update depth exceeded" the moment a previously-failed task started.

Replace the per-component state stack (one useState + one WS
subscription per consumer per task) with a single zustand store
keyed by taskId. task-stream-bootstrap, which already owns the
app-wide WS subscriptions, now writes liveStatus / liveSessionId /
liveFailReason on TaskStarted / TaskCompleted / TaskFailed (with the
same reason -> error -> message fallback chain and synthetic-null
preservation as the panel store). useTaskStatus is a thin selector
that derives the user-facing failReason from either the live store
value or the persisted execution_notes - no setState during render,
single source of truth across TaskPreview and RunTaskButton.

Also fold execution_notes into the TaskOutputSection "Copy all
output" payload only when it carries content distinct from
failReason, so the markdown blob no longer emits the same paragraph
twice on a failed task.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: hoist live task status into shared zustand store

rawCommits.subject: refactor(agents): remove external CLI adapter pathway, leave only aura_harness
rawCommits.body:
External CLI agent adapters (claude_code, codex, gemini_cli, opencode, cursor)
have been deleted from the runtime, validation, UI, evals, scripts, and docs.
Aura OS now only supports the `aura_harness` runtime, which is the path that
actually integrates with the skills system, system-prompt construction, and
provider-managed billing. The `adapter_type` column stays on the agent record
as a sentinel that always defaults to `aura_harness` so we can re-introduce
adapters later without another migration. Pre-release agents that were saved
with an external adapter are intentionally left to break rather than carry
migration code that no callers exercise.

Notable removals:
- runtime.rs: external streaming/parsing helpers, project-tool projection
- crud.rs: explicit rejection of any non-`aura_harness` adapter, simplified
  auth-source matrix (only `aura_managed` and `org_integration`)
- workspace_tools.rs: dead workspace-tool definitions for external adapters
- chat.rs: external chat persistence path, leaving aura_harness as the only
  routing target
- entities.rs: `effective_auth_source` no longer falls back to `local_cli_auth`
- desktop/main.rs: external harness `/health` policy gap is now fatal at boot
  rather than warn-and-continue
- interface integrationCatalog/MODEL_RUNTIME_ADAPTERS pruned to a single Aura
  adapter; `getAdapterLabel`/auth-label helpers collapse accordingly
- AgentEditorModal/useAgentEditorForm hard-codes `aura_harness` and drops the
  local-CLI auth + adapter-scoped integration memory plumbing
- ChatInputBar/ChatPanel/useChatPanelState: dropped per-adapter model menus
  (CODEX/GEMINI/OPENCODE/CURSOR) and codex-specific runtime-model handling
- evals/scripts: deleted external-agent-benchmark scripts, fixtures, and docs;
  cross-adapter project-parity eval; runtime/live eval scripts now assume
  aura_harness only

Tests:
- Added `external_adapters_are_rejected` for the validation path; existing
  unit and integration tests pass under cargo + the touched vitest files.

Made-with: Cursor
rawCommits.cleanSubject: remove external CLI adapter pathway, leave only aura_harness

rendered.title: Harness consolidation, autonomous loop reliability, and a polished desktop shell
rendered.intro: A heavy day for Aura's autonomy stack: the dev loop was hardened against transient provider errors, push failures, and policy denials, then largely refactored to delegate work to the new harness automatons. The desktop shell got a coordinated visual pass with floating capsules and rounded panels, and the chat surface gained richer retry, recovery, and error-state handling. Release tooling also got a real fix after the harness/aura-node crate move.

entries.title: Durable sync checkpoints for the dev loop
entries.summary: Sync milestones now persist across push timeouts and reloads so locally completed work survives restarts, with light typographic polish to the left menu and Sidekick.

items.text: Dev-loop sync milestones and task sync state are now persisted as durable checkpoints, so a push timeout or restart resumes from the last commit/push milestone instead of treating repo sync as opaque task output and surfacing a misleading task failure.
Lightened child-row font weight in the left menu tree and Sidekick so nested items read as clearly subordinate to their parents.

entries.title: Black drag-resize fill on Windows desktop
entries.summary: The Windows desktop window now flashes black instead of white during live drag-resize, blending into Aura's dark theme.

items.text: Switched the main window class background brush back to BLACK_BRUSH so newly exposed client area during drag-resize fills with black, matching the WebView2 background and avoiding a jarring white flash on Windows.

entries.title: Autonomous dev loop hardening and the harness migration
entries.summary: A long arc that turned the dev loop into a recoverable system — DoD guardrails, transient-error retries, push decoupling, ENOSPC handling — and then refactored agent runtime, dev loop, and process scheduling onto the new aura-os-harness automatons.

items.text: Definition-of-Done gate hardened end to end: empty-path writes are rejected unless a real-path write later lands, files_changed is now inferred from successful tool events, workspace preflight runs before the loop starts, and rolled-back commits surface in the UI with a strike-through Git step.

Transient provider errors (HTTP 5xx, stream-terminated, broken pipes) are now classified as retryable with jittered, escalating per-class cooldowns; single-task retries get a budget of 3 to absorb a provider blip, and completion-gate failures route through the same infra retry ladder when the underlying cause was transient.

Retried tasks now ship with a stateful resume preamble ("[aura-retry attempt=N]…") so the next turn doesn't redo work, and the Run pane resolves abandoned tool cards on retry, surfaces task failureReason, and renders a live "Writing retrying (n/8)…" state from the harness's new ToolCallRetrying / ToolCallFailed events with a matching server-side per-tool retry budget.

Task terminal status is now decoupled from git push success — push failures emit a push_deferred event and a project_push_stuck banner instead of demoting completed tasks, with first-class handling for orbit ENOSPC (15-minute cooldown, amber Orbit status dot, runbook) and a remote-push-recovery runbook for operators.

Closed the run_command policy-denial trap end to end: the desktop sidecar now sets AURA_ALLOWED_COMMANDS plus AURA_ALLOW_SHELL, --external-harness probes the harness /health policy, the DoD gate emits a specific diagnostic naming AURA_STRICT_MODE / ENABLE_CMD_TOOLS / binary_allowlist, and aura-harness's run_command-by-default contract is honored across server, sidecar, and docs.

Project-scoped agent tools (create_spec, create_task, list_specs, …) work without the LLM threading project_id: chat sessions auto-splice ReadProject/WriteProject self-caps for the bound project, the cross-agent dispatcher injects project_id from X-Aura-Project-Id, and the schemas drop it from required.

A pure recovery-action reconciler (adopt run, retry push, retry task, decompose, mark terminal) is now exposed on the task-output API as a recommended_action, and a new DoD retry tier with a language-neutral follow-up prompt re-prompts on missing build/test/fmt/lint axes before falling through to terminal failure.

Task state machine is now unified with aura-storage via a single safe_transition helper: the dev loop's five direct transition sites and /tasks/:id/retry all bridge ready→failed via in_progress, /retry is idempotent on ready, and the harness reader WebSocket is closed on every restart so the 128-slot WS cap stops getting exhausted.

Stuck-agent storms are no longer self-perpetuating: terminal harness signals like "appears stuck" or "stopping to prevent waste" now bypass the error-event restart, and provider credit exhaustion is classified as terminal so automation halts immediately with an insufficient-credits outcome.

Major refactor: the legacy agent-tool dispatcher and aura-os-link compatibility layer are deleted, dev loop and process scheduling now run as harness automatons, the reconciler derives recovery from harness signals, and external CLI adapters (claude_code, codex, gemini_cli, opencode, cursor) are retired — Aura OS ships only on the aura_harness runtime.

Concurrent loops are now isolated end to end: a new aura-os-events / aura-os-loops pair tracks chat, automation, task, process and spec loops per (user, project, agent_instance, agent, kind) with topic-scoped channels, the in-memory caches are re-keyed to remove cross-agent bleed, and a single LoopProgress component renders activity in agent rows, the sidekick, and per-task explorer rows from a /api/loops snapshot plus WS bridge.

Desktop shell got a coordinated visual pass: the bottom taskbar splits into three floating glass capsules with consistent 5px insets and rounded corners, the topbar and main content area float with matching radius and gaps, and the right sidekick mirrors the left menu's bottom fade.

Chat and Run pane stream behavior is much steadier: scroll uses overflow-anchor instead of MutationObserver-driven sentinels (eliminating the up-then-down blink), mid-turn state is recovered from the server after a hard refresh, the assistant turn is preserved at end-of-stream, and stale "active" panel rows in localStorage are reconciled against the server's active task list.

Browser surface gets a flat Edge-style address pill, free-form queries fall back to a DuckDuckGo search, and main-frame load failures now render an Aura-branded error overlay with Ask Agent / Show Details / Reload instead of Chromium's default error page.

Native desktop polish: macOS gains a real NSMenu so Cmd+C/V/X/A/Z work inside the WebView, the custom titlebar is now draggable on macOS and Linux via tao::drag_window (WebKit ignores -webkit-app-region), and the chat composer is hidden until history resolves to avoid an empty-agent flash.
Inline rename for project agents: a Rename entry in the sidebar context menu (and F2 on a focused row) opens the existing inline rename overlay, matching the project rename UX.

Spec and file previews gained a Copy button with cross-OS clipboard support (navigator.clipboard with execCommand fallback for Capacitor WebViews), and the GroupCollapsible header was fixed so interactive controls no longer cause a nested-button hydration error.

Tool call inputs that arrived as stringified JSON no longer render as character-indexed objects, and historical/inline [tool: …] markers in completed task output are now expanded into real Block renderers instead of falling back to the legacy inline marker path.

entries.title: Desktop sidecar build fix after the harness package move
entries.summary: The release script that bundles aura-node into the desktop sidecar was failing because the binary now lives in the aura-runtime package; the build invocation was corrected.

items.text: prepare-desktop-sidecar now builds aura-node with `-p aura-runtime --bin aura-node` instead of `-p aura-node`, fixing the "no bin target named aura-node in default-run packages" error introduced when the binary moved into the aura-runtime package.

entries.title: Reverted accidental hooks/ relocation
entries.summary: A CI retrigger commit unintentionally moved interface/src/hooks into interface/src/shared/hooks without updating imports; it was reverted to restore the build.

items.text: A workflow-retrigger change accidentally moved interface/src/hooks/* under interface/src/shared/hooks/ without rewriting imports, breaking main; the original layout was restored to unblock CI.

entries.title: Changelog media capture workflow guards
entries.summary: The release-changelog media workflow gained tighter skip and capture gates, plus matching planner/quality logic and small shell adjustments.

items.text: Hardened the publish-release-changelog-media workflow with stricter skip and capture gates and matching planner/quality logic so changelog media generation no longer runs (or fails) on releases that shouldn't produce it.

highlights: Autonomous dev loop now survives provider 5xx, push failures, and policy denials
Major refactor: agent runtime, dev loop, and process scheduling now run through the unified harness
Desktop shell redesigned with floating capsules, rounded panels, and corner-radius cohesion
Chat and run pane preserve in-flight state across refresh and surface live tool retry status
External CLI agent adapters retired — Aura OS now ships only on the aura_harness runtime
